middleware.description: Refuses actions that require an active-status staff row. Applied to routes that touch a Staff whose employment_status must be `active` (coach-create against a pre_hire staff is refused; branch-manager assignment on a suspended staff is refused). Resolves the target Staff from the route parameter (`{staff}`) and returns 422 `STAFF_NOT_ACTIVE` when employment_status != 'active'. Distinct from the observer refusal — the middleware fails fast at the route boundary so the caller doesn't burn a request through the full pipeline for a known-bad state.
auto_applied_to: /coaches (POST — coach create requires active parent staff)

middleware.description: Field-level permission enforcer for the compensation sub-resource. Refuses the whole `/staff/{staff}/compensation` endpoint (GET + PATCH) when the caller lacks `staff.view.compensation` (for GET) or `staff.manage.compensation` (for PATCH). Also refuses when the tenant has not enabled the `staff_compensation_tracking` entitlement. This is separate from the field-level redaction that applies on the parent `/staff/{staff}` GET — the sub-resource is entirely permission-gated so the middleware can 403 the request before any DB fetch.

consumed.note: The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json. Route-level `permission` metadata resolves through this middleware.

ResolveScope narrows queries by the caller's active branch scope. Applied globally to authenticated tenant routes; staff routes rely on it for the branch narrowing described in policies.json (a branch_manager sees their branch's staff only).
